assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Hi Andrey, saw the explainability gap around contractor payouts. Mellow gives finance a cleaner payment trail, KYC records, and auditable documents for the part we operate. It does not remove misclassification risk outside the real working relationship. If helpful, I can outline the control boundary in 3 lines.
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.

It does not remove misclassification risk outside the real working relationship. If helpful, I can outline the control boundary in 3 lines.
criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: Hi Andrey, saw the explainability gap around contractor payouts. Mellow gives finance a cleaner payment trail, KYC records, and auditable documents for the part we operate. objections.objection: Fine. Straight to it: what's in your scope, what's not?

criteria.label: Core objection handling

criteria.name: Next step
criteria.short_reason: Conversation ended without a clear next step.
criteria.evidence_quote: In scope: contractor onboarding workflow, KYC, payment routing inside Mellow, documents, and audit trail. Out of scope: the factual nature of the client-contractor relationship outside the platform. If a payout gets stuck, there is an accountable support path and traceable status instead of ad hoc chasing through intermediaries.
criteria.label: Clear next step

transcript.text: Hi Andrey, saw the explainability gap around contractor payouts. Mellow gives finance a cleaner payment trail, KYC records, and auditable documents for the part we operate. It does not remove misclassification risk outside the real working relationship. If helpful, I can outline the control boundary in 3 lines.
Fine. Straight to it: what's in your scope, what's not?

In scope: contractor onboarding workflow, KYC, payment routing inside Mellow, documents, and audit trail. Out of scope: the factual nature of the client-contractor relationship outside the platform. If a payout gets stuck, there is an accountable support path and traceable status instead of ad hoc chasing through intermediaries.
